payload.text: I’ll inspect the current Codex service and backend test setup first, then make the smallest shared resolver change that keeps the public status shape intact.
zsh:1: command not found: rg

`rg` is not installed in this environment, so I’m falling back to targeted `grep`/`find`. The main service currently constructs `Codex` without an override, and status only probes `codex` from PATH, which matches the ticket’s findings.

{
  "name": "relay",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "description": "Local-first Codex-driven kanban desktop app.",
  "main": "./out/main/index.js",
  "type": "module",
  "private": true,
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "electron-vite dev",
    "dev:logs": "electron-vite dev 2>&1 | tee /tmp/relay-dev.log",
    "build": "tsc --noEmit && electron-vite build",
    "clean:dist": "node scripts/package-electron.mjs --clean",
    "package:binary": "node scripts/package-electron.mjs",
    "dist": "npm run clean:dist && npm run build && npm run package:binary",
    "logs": "mkdir -p \"$HOME/Library/Application Support/relay\" && touch \"$HOME/Library/Application Support/relay/relay.log\" && tail -n 200 -f \"$HOME/Library/Application Support/relay/relay.log\"",
    "logs:dev": "touch /tmp/relay-dev.log && tail -n 200 -f /tmp/relay-dev.log",
    "preview": "electron-vite preview",
    "test": "node tests/run-tests.mjs",
    "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@dnd-kit/core": "^6.3.1",
    "@dnd-kit/sortable": "^10.0.0",
    "@dnd-kit/utilities": "^3.2.2",
    "@openai/codex-sdk": "^0.130.0",
    "clsx": "^2.1.1",
    "effect": "4.0.0-beta.65",
    "gray-matter": "^4.0.3",
    "lucide-react": "^0.468.0",
    "react": "^19.0.0",
    "react-dom": "^19.0.0",
    "ulid": "^2.3.0"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@types/node": "^22.10.2",
    "@types/react": "^19.0.2",
    "@types/react-dom": "^19.0.2",
    "@vitejs/plugin-react": "^4.3.4",
    "electron": "^33.2.1",
    "electron-vite": "^2.3.0",
    "typescript": "^5.7.2",
    "vite": "^5.4.11"
  }
}

The test runner bundles tests with a shim for `@openai/codex-sdk`, so I’ll add tests around exported helpers rather than trying to instantiate the real SDK in the bundled test process. I’m checking the dependency seams around preflight and client creation before editing.

payload.text:
src/main/services/codex/status.ts:36:        : "Codex CLI is available, but no Codex auth file or API key was found."
src/main/services/codex/status.ts:37:      : "Codex CLI was not found on PATH."
src/main/services/codex/index.ts:512:        "Codex CLI was not found on PATH. Install or expose Codex before drafting tickets.",
src/main/services/codex/index.ts:513:        "codex_cli_unavailable"

I’m going to add a `cli` helper beside the Codex service. It will own candidate ordering, bundled-path resolution, and the `--version` probing loop, then both status and `createCodex` will call that same helper.
The shared resolver is in place. Next I’m wiring status to consume it while leaving the auth-file/API-key branch intact.
I’m updating runtime creation now. The internal factory will resolve the executable with the same helper and pass it as `codexPathOverride`, so SDK launch behavior matches the status probe.
The runtime error text for drafting now reflects both lookup paths. I’m adding focused backend tests around the resolver/status seam and the Codex factory override.
The tests now cover bundled success, PATH fallback, total failure, and the factory override path. I’m doing a quick static read of the changed files before running the full suite.
The changed code is scoped and the call sites all await the async factory now. I’m running typecheck first to catch signature or bundling issues before the slower test suite.

> relay@0.1.0 typecheck
> tsc --noEmit

tests/backend.test.ts(191,33): error TS2339: Property 'codexPathOverride' does not exist on type 'never'.
tests/backend.test.ts(192,37): error TS2339: Property 'env' does not exist on type 'never'.
Typecheck found only a test narrowing issue: TypeScript cannot prove the injected factory callback ran. I’m adjusting that test to store captured options in an array and assert the entry exists.

> relay@0.1.0 typecheck
> tsc --noEmit
`npm run typecheck` passes after the test adjustment. I’m running the full test suite now.

The test bundle warns because the new helper uses `import.meta.url` while tests emit CJS. I’m waiting for the suite result; if that warning becomes a runtime problem, I’ll switch the resolver initialization to a CJS-safe pattern.

`npm test` exposed two real integration issues in the new helper: the top-level `createRequire(import.meta.url)` breaks the CJS test bundle, and the repo’s import-boundary test flags raw Node IO imports in service modules. I’m moving those concerns behind existing backend services/helpers.

The helper now uses the repo’s `io/path` helpers and only creates a module resolver lazily, using `__filename` in the CJS test bundle and `import.meta.url` in the ESM app build. I’m re-running typecheck.

> relay@0.1.0 typecheck
> tsc --noEmit
`npm run typecheck` passes again. I’m rerunning `npm test`; the previous failure was from the test bundle and import-boundary checks, so this should verify both fixes.

The full test suite passes now. There is a non-fatal esbuild warning from the CJS test bundle about the ESM-only `import.meta.url` fallback; runtime behavior is covered and the command exited successfully. I’m checking the final diff and file list.

?? src/main/services/codex/cli.ts

Summary of changes made:
- Added a shared Codex CLI resolver that prefers the SDK-bundled `@openai/codex` binary and falls back to PATH `codex`.
- Updated `getCodexStatus()` and `createCodex()` to use the same resolved executable.
- Passed `codexPathOverride` into `new Codex({ codexPathOverride, env })`.
- Kept `CodexStatus` shape unchanged.
- Added backend tests for bundled success, PATH fallback, total failure, and runtime override behavior.

Files changed:
- `src/main/services/codex/cli.ts`
- `src/main/services/codex/status.ts`
- `src/main/services/codex/index.ts`
- `tests/backend.test.ts`

Commands run:
- `npm run typecheck`
- `npm test`
- Plus targeted inspection commands with `sed`, `grep`, `find`, `cat`, and `git diff/status`.

Tests run and results:
- `npm run typecheck`: passed.
- `npm test`: passed, 91/91 tests. It emits a non-fatal esbuild warning about `import.meta.url` in the CJS test bundle.

Remaining risks or follow-up:
- `git status` shows unrelated `.relay/*` working tree changes; I left them untouched.
- No required follow-up for this ticket.

payload.finalResponse:
Summary of changes made:
- Added a shared Codex CLI resolver that prefers the SDK-bundled `@openai/codex` binary and falls back to PATH `codex`.
- Updated `getCodexStatus()` and `createCodex()` to use the same resolved executable.
- Passed `codexPathOverride` into `new Codex({ codexPathOverride, env })`.
- Kept `CodexStatus` shape unchanged.
- Added backend tests for bundled success, PATH fallback, total failure, and runtime override behavior.

Files changed:
- `src/main/services/codex/cli.ts`
- `src/main/services/codex/status.ts`
- `src/main/services/codex/index.ts`
- `tests/backend.test.ts`

Commands run:
- `npm run typecheck`
- `npm test`
- Plus targeted inspection commands with `sed`, `grep`, `find`, `cat`, and `git diff/status`.

Tests run and results:
- `npm run typecheck`: passed.
- `npm test`: passed, 91/91 tests. It emits a non-fatal esbuild warning about `import.meta.url` in the CJS test bundle.

Remaining risks or follow-up:
- `git status` shows unrelated `.relay/*` working tree changes; I left them untouched.
- No required follow-up for this ticket.
